items.title: Australia bans neo-Nazi network under new law that criminalises hate groups
items.summary: The National Socialist Network, also known as White Australia, said it would disband after the government in January passed the law allowing certain organisations to be banned

items.title: War worsens Lebanon's economic crisis with job losses, slow business, soaring prices
items.summary: Lebanon suffered some $70 billion in losses in its financial sector, further compounded by about $11 billion in the 2024 war between Israel and Hezbollah, according to World Bank

items.title: Two killed as heavy rains batter Sri Lanka; thousands affected
items.summary: More than 4,000 people have been affected, with more than 100 people from 29 families being placed in two temporary relief centres

items.title: CIA director visits Cuba as island nation runs out of oil
items.summary: The Central Intelligence Agency, at the heart of the decades-long struggle between the United States and Cuba, confirmed a Cuban government statement about Director John Ratcliffe's visit

items.title: U.K. Health Secretary resigns, setting up potential Labour leadership challenge to Starmer

items.title: Watch: Congress ends suspense: V. D. Satheesan to be next Kerala CM | Above the Fold | 14.05.2026
items.summary: In today’s episode: Kerala finally gets its next Chief Minister as the Congress names V.D. Satheesan for the top post after weeks of suspense and internal discussions. We also track the BRICS Foreign Ministers’ meeting hosted by India in New Delhi, where External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar addressed key global challenges and diplomatic priorities.

items.title: COVID-19 linked to 22.1 million excess deaths globally between 2020 and 2023: WHO report
items.summary: World Health Statistics report says the pandemic reversed a decade of gains in life expectancy and exposed deep vulnerabilities in global health systems, even as progress was recorded in HIV, sanitation, and disease control

items.title: In Iran’s holy city of Qom, Indian volunteers serve sharbat amid war fears and protests
items.summary: For many here, it is a symbol — that while governments may change positions and diplomacy may fluctuate, ordinary people can still choose compassion

items.title: Iran turns bombed Karaj Bridge into a symbol of resistance
items.summary: The B1 corridor connects Tehran with northern and northwestern regions that have large Azeri populations. Karaj itself has a substantial Azeri community, while cities further northwest such as Tabriz are overwhelmingly Azeri

items.title: Sleepless in Tehran as crowds rally behind the flag
items.summary: A slogan on a wall nearby in Farsi read, “Meydan bashuma, khayabane ba ma”. ‘The battlefield is yours. The streets are ours.’ Addressed to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), it was a message to the soldiers to focus on the battlefield with ordinary Iranians in control of their homes

items.title: Ship anchored off east coast of UAE seized, heading toward Iranian waters

items.title: What is Trump’s Project Vault?
items.summary: The Trump administration’s initiative aims to create a reserve of critical minerals to protect its domestic industries from any disruption, as witnessed when China restricted export of rare earth magnets to retaliate against U.S. tariffs, causing a near shutdown of car manufacturing operations in the U.S. and the world over

items.title: UAE 'denies reports' of secret Netanyahu visit

items.title: Watch: What to expect from the Trump-Xi summit
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump lands in Beijing for a three-day summit with President Xi. The two leaders are expected to discuss trade, the war in Iran, the technology race and more. The Hindu’s China correspondent, Ananth Krishnan, breaks down what we can expect from the meeting, and what needs to happen for the two sides to declare this meeting a success.

items.title: Iran Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrives in India, to attend BRICS FM meet
items.summary: Araghchi will be coming face to face with UAE Minister of State for Foreign Affairs on Thursday (May 14, 2026) at BRICS Foreign Ministers summit
